mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly rise in the unemployment rate signals immediate labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by > 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 55% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal spending and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in spending and housing fails to predict positive quarterly returns with a Rank IC above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth shows zero predictive power for 20-day returns during early-cycle periods, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: High inflation months are followed by positive SPX returns in > 60% of cases, invalidating the tightening fear channel.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirm a robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer confidence and yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative forward returns in > 50% of triggered months over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects strong aggregate economic activity and corporate revenue expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth rates exhibit a Rank IC below 0.02 for 5-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly contractions in industrial production signal a supply-side slowdown and weakening business investment, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances following two consecutive months of industrial production declines.
